title: Express 5 Routing, Path Syntax, Parameters, and Dispatch · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A municipal incident-response team and public client developers. They need an outcome-driven TypeScript service with a reproducible Node toolchain, explicit lifecycle, correct HTTP semantics, Node protocol limits, and an unambiguous Express 5 route table. Planned artifact: Deliver Reproducible Node and Express Civic Service Core with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, and recovery evidence for a service outcome before choosing endpoints.
Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.
Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Project Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
project case — A project team handles case 5002 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then replace an average latency claim with p95 and event-loop saturation evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, and recovery evidence for a service outcome before choosing endpoints.

Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Route Table Gate happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Route Table Gate
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Route Table Gate: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A project team handles case 9943 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Route Table Gate.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Route Table Gate, keep Route Table Gate connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.

hints: For Node and Express server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Route Table Gate, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Route Table Gate precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Route Table Gate, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A project team handles case 9943 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.

For Node and Express server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Route Table Gate, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: If each intended endpoint succeeds once, routing is complete and conflict free.
